reasoning:
To reach a rating at least 1% higher than his 2025-05-11 rating of 2738, Anish Giri must attain a rating of essentially 2766 or higher (+28 Elo).
1. For 2025-05-18: FIDE officially updates its classical rankings on the 1st of every month, and Wikipedia strictly mirrors these official snapshots. The likelihood that the May 18 Wikipedia table contains a different mid-month classical rating is negligible. Thus, the May 18 rating will stand at 2738 (a 0% increase).
2. For 2025-06-10: The June list will be published. Gaining 28 points in a single month equates to an immense +5.6 net score over expectation against Super GM opposition. Giri's solid, rarely losing but heavily drawing style makes swinging 28 points in one month practically impossible.
3. For longer horizons (August and November): While regaining a 2766 classical rating is historically within his capabilities (having hovered around 2760-2775 frequently in the past few years), it requires stringing together several outstanding performances (+2 to +3) back-to-back across multiple rating periods. His multi-year downtrend bounds this probability, though chances naturally climb as the time horizon extends.
